talk_metadata.talk_title: Multiplex Assays of Variant Effects (MAVEs) for BRCA2 and other breast/ovarian cancer predisposition genes
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We don't always know that this haploid cell is behaving the right way. Is it behaving the same as a diploid cell?"
Speaker said: "MAVEs are not perfect. There is error in MAVEs, and you can see that there's a few percent that are differential, that are wrong"
Speaker said: "We expect that in a biological system. Perhaps more replicates would have fixed that"
Speaker said: "We are still only doing function. This is not clinical classification"

key_data_gaps: No OS or clinical outcome data presented
Lack of splicing/ RNA-level validation for many variants
Uncertainty about cell‑type specific effects (HAP1 vs primary mammary cells)

crispro_opportunity.description: Current MAVEs lack systematic splicing readouts; integrating RNA‑seq based splicing metrics would fill this gap.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We put in 10 bases on each side of the canonical splice site... there are a lot of variants that do seem to knock out activity... but there’s no mention of any RNA work"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model variant impact on splicing using deep learning of RNA‑seq data and combine with DNA‑based functional scores.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: studies on splicing effects of BRCA2 variants
ClinicalTrials.gov: any trials using splicing‑modulating therapies for BRCA2

crispro_opportunity.description: Integrate polygenic risk scores (PRS) with MAVE‑derived functional classifications to improve individualized risk prediction.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We have not looked at it yet. It's simply an idea on a piece of paper... PRS modifies different mutations"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can overlay PRS calculations with variant‑specific functional probabilities to generate a composite risk metric.
external_validation_needed: UK Biobank: genotype‑phenotype data for BRCA2 carriers

crispro_opportunity.description: Design prospective trials that stratify patients by MAVE‑derived functional categories rather than only by protein‑truncating status.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We end up with 608 that remain as VUS... over 95% of the variants... combining MAVEs dramatically improves diagnostics"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial power under different functional stratifications to optimize enrollment criteria.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: identify ongoing BRCA2‑targeted therapy trials

cited_competitors.name: AlphaMissense
cited_competitors.context: Benchmarking computational predictor against MAVE functional scores

cited_competitors.name: ClinVar
cited_competitors.context: Source of known pathogenic/benign standards for calibration

cited_competitors.name: Ambry Genetics
cited_competitors.context: Provided case‑control data for risk association and standards

unresolved_questions: How do variant effects differ between HAP1 cells and primary mammary epithelial cells?
What proportion of VUS are truly pathogenic versus assay artefacts?
Can a single viability‑based MAVE capture all disease‑relevant mechanisms (e.g., replication‑fork stress)?

talk_metadata.talk_title: MAVEs for Lynch syndrome mismatch‑repair genes and colorectal cancer risk
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have now completed variant‑to‑function maps of all four of the key MMR genes. Only three would fit on the slide here, but this adds up to over 73,000 variants"
Speaker said: "One key thing we did… was to gather a gold‑standard set of clinical variants… and for 47 of those 48 variants, our functional data agreed directionally"
Speaker said: "AlphaMissense provides an output which is a so‑called probability of pathogenicity… you can see there's a lot more red on the bottom"
Speaker said: "We are interested in exploring whether we can use MAIV data to fine‑tune or improve computational predictions"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Over‑calling pathogenicity because evolutionary constraints do not map directly to cancer risk in humans

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on a single drug‑induced phenotype; may miss variants that affect other MMR functions without altering 6TG sensitivity
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing a broad viability readout may dilute signal for hypomorphic variants

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that AlphaMissense predicts >40% pathogenicity for PMS2 missense variants, but the MAVE functional assay shows only ~12% loss‑of‑function – yet the speaker concluded that computational predictors are “getting better and better” without reconciling this discrepancy

key_data_gaps: Limited clinical phasing data for double‑mutant haplotypes
No systematic database of paired missense variants in patients
Splicing‑specific MAVEs not yet integrated for MMR genes

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify and model epistatic interactions between mild missense variants (e.g., K618A) that together push functional scores into pathogenic range.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We are making all these other variants on the background of K618A… a substantial minority of missense variants are poised for functional abnormality, and then when they occur on this background… they’re sort of pushed down the sledding hill"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict combinatorial effects using a probabilistic epistasis model trained on the double‑mutant MAVE data.

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop RNA‑seq based splicing MAVEs for MMR genes to complement the viability assay and capture splice‑disrupting VUS.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "There’s no mention of any RNA work. Nobody’s done that since the 2018 BRCA1 paper"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s transcript‑impact module can integrate splice‑junction quantification with DNA‑based functional scores.
external_validation_needed: Published splicing MAVEs for other DNA‑repair genes
GTEx RNA‑seq data for MMR gene expression

crispro_opportunity.description: Use MAVE‑derived loss‑of‑function classifications to stratify Lynch syndrome patients in existing surveillance trials.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Our functional data allowed us to retrospectively reclassify 10 individuals as Lynch syndrome"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate risk scores that combine functional classification with family‑history models for trial eligibility.

cited_competitors.name: AlphaMissense
cited_competitors.context: Benchmarking against functional MAPs for PMS2

cited_competitors.name: ClinVar
cited_competitors.context: Source of gold‑standard clinical variants for calibration

cited_competitors.name: Ambry Genetics
cited_competitors.context: Provided case‑control risk data and variant standards

unresolved_questions: What is the clinical prevalence of the K618A‑background epistatic interactions?
How do missense variants that affect splicing versus protein stability differ in cancer risk?
Can a single viability assay capture all pathogenic mechanisms of MMR deficiency?

assets_to_track: MMR gene MAVEs (MSH2, MLH1, MSH6, PMS2)
